name: funcnodes-core
documentation:
Core package for funcnodes.
for detailed instructions go to the funcnodes repo

classes.name: funcnodes_core.exceptions.NodeIdAlreadyExistsError
documentation: Exception raised when a node ID already exists.

functions.name: funcnodes_core.decorator.update_other_io_options
documentation:
Generate a callback function that updates the value options of on ot multiple other ios from the same node.

Args:
    trg_io (Union[str,List[str]]): The name of the target io or a list of multiple ios.
    modifier (Optional[Callable[[Any], Any]], optional): A function that modifies the result before
    updating the value options. Defaults to identity.

Returns:
    Callable[[fn.NodeIO, Any], None]: A callback function that updates the value options of the target io.
        The first argument is the source io, the second argument is the respective value
        (in most cases the value of the src io).

functions.name: funcnodes_core.decorator.update_other_io_value_options
documentation:
Generate a callback function that updates the alue_options of one or multiple other ios from the same node.

Args:
    trg_io (Union[str,List[str]]): The name of the target io or a list of multiple ios.
    options_generator (Optional[Callable[[Any], ValueOptions]], optional): A function that
        generates the new value options, applied to all trg_ios.

Returns:
    Callable[[fn.NodeIO, Any], None]: A callback function that updates the value options of the target io.
        The first argument is the source io, the second argument is the respective value
        (in most cases the value of the src io).

classes.name: funcnodes_core.data.DataEnum
documentation:
Base class for data enums. They should be used as a type hint for a function argument for funcnodes.
In the function the value can be accessed by using the v method.
The reson for this is to be more robust that the values to the function can be
passed as the Enum, as a value or as a enum key.

Example:
```python
class TestEnum(DataEnum):
    A = 1
    B = 2
    C = 3

@NodeDecorator(node_id="test_enum")
def test_enum_node(a: TestEnum) -> int:
    a = TestEnum.v(a)
    return a

functions.name: funcnodes_core.nodemaker.node_class_maker
documentation:
Creates a node class from a function.

Args:
  id (str, optional): The id of the node. Defaults to None.
  func (Callable[..., ReturnType], optional): The function to be wrapped. Defaults to None.
  superclass (Type[Node], optional): The superclass of the node. Defaults to Node.
  **kwargs (Unpack[NodeClassDict]): Keyword arguments for the node class.

Returns:
  Type[Node]: The created node class.

Raises:
  ValueError: If the superclass is not a subclass of Node.
  ValueError: If the node_id is not set.

functions.name: funcnodes_core.nodemaker.NodeDecorator
documentation: Decorator to create a Node class from a function.

functions.name: funcnodes_core.nodemaker.instance_nodefunction
documentation:
Decorator for creating instance node functions.

Args:
  trigger_on_call (bool, optional): Whether to trigger the node when the
    underlying NodeClassMixin-function is called.
    If None, the node will be triggered if it has no inputs.
    Defaults to None.
  **kwargs (Unpack[NodeDecoratorKwargs]): Keyword arguments for the decorator.

Returns:
  Callable: The decorated function.

Raises:
  ValueError: If the function is not an instance_nodefunction.

functions.name: funcnodes_core.nodemaker.trigger_decorator
documentation:
A decorator that triggers the corresponding nodes when the function is called.

Args:
  target_func: A function wrapped in instance_nodefunction.

Returns:
  Callable: The decorated function.

Raises:
  ValueError: If the function is not an instance_nodefunction.

Examples:
  >>> class MyNodeClass(NodeClassMixin):
  >>>   NODECLASSID = "my_node_class"
  >>>
  >>>   @instance_nodefunction
  >>>   def add(self, a, b):
  >>>     return a + b
  >>>
  >>>   @add.triggers
  >>>   def eval(self, a, b):
  >>>     # calling this function will trigger the add nodes for this instance

class_variables.name: funcnodes_core.nodemaker.NodeClassMixin.NODECLASSID
documentation: type: ignore

The NodeClassMixin can be used on any class to
transform its methods into node classes.
Each instance of the class will have its own Nodeclassess,
making them independend from each other.
This is especially useful for creating nodes that are
bound to each other in a specific way, which can be mediated
by the respective class.

Attributes:
  NODECLASSID (str): The unique id of the class, forwardet to the node.
  IS_ABSTRACT (bool): Whether the node class is abstract or not.

Examples:
  >>> class MyNodeClass(NodeClassMixin):
  >>>   NODECLASSID = "my_node_class"
  >>>
  >>>   @instance_nodefunction
  >>>   def add(self, a, b):
  >>>     return a + b
  >>>
  >>>   @add.triggers
  >>>   def eval(self, a, b):
  >>>     # calling this function will trigger the add nodes for this instance

classes.name: funcnodes_core.triggerstack.TriggerStack

methods.name: funcnodes_core.triggerstack.TriggerStack.__init__
documentation: Initializes a new instance of TriggerStack with an empty stack.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.triggerstack.TriggerStack.append
documentation:
Appends a new asyncio.Task to the stack.

Args:
    node (asyncio.Task): The asyncio.Task to append to the stack.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.triggerstack.TriggerStack.check
documentation: Checks the stack and removes any completed tasks from the end of the stack.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.triggerstack.TriggerStack.done
documentation:
Checks if all tasks in the stack are done.

Returns:
    bool: True if all tasks are done, False otherwise.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.triggerstack.TriggerStack.__await__
documentation:
Allows an instance of TriggerStack to be awaited. This will await the last task
in the stack repeatedly until the stack is empty, meaning all tasks are done.

Yields:
    The result of awaiting the last task in the stack.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.triggerstack.TriggerStack.__len__
documentation:
Returns the number of tasks currently in the stack.

Returns:
    int: The number of tasks in the stack.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.triggerstack.TriggerStack.__getitem__
documentation:
Gets the task at the specified index in the stack.

Args:
    index (int): The index of the task to retrieve.

Returns:
    asyncio.Task: The task at the specified index.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.triggerstack.TriggerStack.__aiter__
documentation:
Make the TriggerStack an asynchronous iterable.

Returns:
    An asynchronous iterator over the results of the tasks in the stack.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.triggerstack.TriggerStack.__anext__
documentation:
Return the next result from the stack or raise StopAsyncIteration
if there are no more tasks.

Returns:
    The result of the next task in the stack.

Raises:
    StopAsyncIteration: If there are no more tasks in the stack.

A class that manages a stack of asyncio.Tasks. It provides functionality to append tasks,
check their completion status, and await the completion of the entire stack of tasks.

functions.name: funcnodes_core.utils.data.deep_fill_dict
documentation:
deep_fill_dict
A target dict is filled with the values of a source dict recursively
if the key does not exist in the target dict

Parameters
----------
target_dict : Dict[Any, Any]
    The dict to be filled
source_dict : dict
    The dict to be used as a source
overwrite_existing : bool, optional
    If true, existing values in the target dict will be overwritten, by default False
inplace : bool, optional
    If true, the target dict is modified in place, by default True

Returns
-------
Dict[Any, Any]
    The filled dict

functions.name: funcnodes_core.utils.data.deep_remove_dict_on_equal
documentation:
deep_remove_dict_on_equal
All keys in a dict that have the same value as the value of the key
in theremove dict are removed

Parameters
----------
target_dict : dict
    The dict to be cleaned
remove_dict : dict
    The dict to be used as a source
inplace : bool, optional
    If true, the target dict is modified in place, by default True

Returns
-------
Dict[Any, Any]
    The cleaned dict

functions.name: funcnodes_core.eventmanager.emit_before
documentation:
A decorator factory that returns a decorator, which when applied to a function,
emits an event before the function has been called.

Parameters
----------
include_kwargs : Union[Literal["all", "none"], List[str]]
    Controls which keyword arguments are included in the message that's emitted.
    - "all": Include all keyword arguments.
    - "none": Include none of the keyword arguments.
    - List[str]: Include only the specified list of keyword argument names.

Returns
-------
Callable
    A decorator that can be applied to a method of a class that inherits from EventEmitterMixin.

functions.name: funcnodes_core.eventmanager.emit_after
documentation:
A decorator factory that returns a decorator, which when applied to a function,
emits an event after the function has been called.

Parameters
----------
include_kwargs : Union[Literal["all", "none"], List[str]]
    Controls which keyword arguments are included in the message that's emitted.
    - "all": Include all keyword arguments.
    - "none": Include none of the keyword arguments.
    - List[str]: Include only the specified list of keyword argument names.
include_result : bool, optional
    Whether to include the result of the function call in the emitted message, by default True.

Returns
-------
Callable
    A decorator that can be applied to a method of a class that inherits from EventEmitterMixin.

Args:

    delta (float): The amount of time to wait before clearing the event in seconds, defaults to 0.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.eventmanager.AsyncEventManager.remove_event
documentation:
Removes the specified event from the event manager.

Args:
    event (str): The name of the event to remove.

Manages asyncio events for a given object, allowing for waiting on, setting,
and clearing events identified by string names.

classes.name: funcnodes_core.eventmanager.EventEmitterMixin

methods.name: funcnodes_core.eventmanager.EventEmitterMixin.__init__
documentation: Initializes a new EventEmitterMixin object.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.eventmanager.EventEmitterMixin.cleanup
documentation: Remove all event listeners and perform necessary cleanup before deletion.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.eventmanager.EventEmitterMixin.__del__
documentation: Cleans up the EventEmitterMixin object upon deletion.
Ensure to call cleanup before the object is deleted

methods.name: funcnodes_core.eventmanager.EventEmitterMixin.on
documentation:
Adds a listener to the end of the listeners array for the specified event.

Parameters
----------
event_name : str
    The name of the event.
callback : EventCallback
    The callback function.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.eventmanager.EventEmitterMixin.on_error
documentation:
Adds a listener to the end of the listeners array for the error event.

Parameters
----------
callback : EventErrorCallback
    The callback function.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.eventmanager.EventEmitterMixin.off
documentation:
removes the specified listener from the listener array for the specified event.
If no callback is passed, all listeners for the event are removed.
If the event is empty after removing the listener, it will be removed as well.

Args:
    event_name (str): The name of the event.
    callback (Optional[EventCallback], optional): The callback function or None (will remove all listeners
    for the event). Defaults to None.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.eventmanager.EventEmitterMixin.off_error
documentation:
removes the specified listener from the listener array for the error event.
If no callback is passed, all listeners for the error event are removed.

Args:
    callback (Optional[EventErrorCallback], optional): The callback function or None (will remove all listeners
    for the error event). Defaults to None.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.eventmanager.EventEmitterMixin.once
documentation:
Adds a one time listener for the event. This listener is invoked
only the next time the event is fired, after which it is removed.

Parameters
----------
event_name : str
    The name of the event.
callback : EventCallback
    The callback function.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.eventmanager.EventEmitterMixin.once_error
documentation:
Adds a one time listener for the error event. This listener is invoked only the next
time the error event is fired, after which it is removed.

Parameters
----------
callback : EventErrorCallback
    The callback function.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.eventmanager.EventEmitterMixin.emit
documentation:
Execute each of the listeners in order with the supplied arguments.

Parameters
----------
event_name : str
    The name of the event.
*args
    The arguments to pass to the listeners.
**kwargs
    The keyword arguments to pass to the listeners.

Returns
-------
bool
    True if the event had listeners, False otherwise.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.eventmanager.EventEmitterMixin.error
documentation:
Emits an error event.
if the error event has listeners, it will call them with the passed error.
if the error event has no listeners, it will raise the passed error.


Parameters
----------
e : Exception
    The error to emit.

Returns
-------
bool
    True if the error event had listeners,
    False otherwise (should not happen since, then it should be raised).

Raises
------
Exception
    Raises the passed Exception of the error event had no listeners.

EventEmitterMixin is a mixin class that provides methods for
emitting and listening to events.

functions.name: funcnodes_core.io.raise_allow_connections
documentation:
Checks and raises an exception if a connection between two NodeIO instances is not allowed.

Args:
    src: The source NodeIO instance.
    trg: The target NodeIO instance.

Returns:
    True if the connection is allowed, otherwise raises an exception.

Raises:
    NodeConnectionError: If attempting to connect two outputs or two inputs.
    MultipleConnectionsError: If either the source or target does not allow
        multiple connections.

functions.name: funcnodes_core.utils.nodeutils.get_deep_connected_nodeset
documentation:
Recursively collects all the nodes that are connected to the source node, including those
connected indirectly through other nodes' outputs. This function traverses the graph of nodes
starting from the source node.

Args:
    src (Node): The source node from which to start the traversal.
    nodeset (Optional[Set[Node]]): The set of nodes already visited in the traversal,
                                         used to avoid cycles and repeated nodes.

Returns:
    Set[Node]: A set containing all the connected nodes including the source node.

functions.name: funcnodes_core.utils.nodeutils.run_until_complete
documentation:
Asynchronously runs a loop that checks if the nodes provided are in a trigger state.
If a node is in a trigger state and requests a trigger, the function will trigger it.
The loop continues until none of the nodes are in a trigger state.

Args:
    *nodes (Node): A variable number of Node instances that need to be run until completion.

Returns:
    None

classes.name: funcnodes_core.utils.special_types.databytes

functions.name: funcnodes_core.utils.functions._make_async
documentation:
Converts a synchronous function into an asynchronous function.

This function wraps a given synchronous function (`func`) in an asynchronous
function that can be awaited. This is useful when you need to integrate
synchronous functions into an asynchronous codebase.

Args:
    func (Callable[P, R]): A synchronous function to be wrapped.

Returns:
    Callable[P, Awaitable[R]]: An asynchronous version of the given function.

Example:
    >>> def sync_function(x: int) -> int:
    ...     return x * 2
    ...
    >>> async_function = _make_async(sync_function)
    >>> result = await async_function(5)
    >>> print(result)
    10

functions.name: funcnodes_core.utils.functions._make_sync
documentation:
Converts an asynchronous function into a synchronous function.

This function wraps a given asynchronous function (`func`) so that it can be
called synchronously. The asynchronous function will be executed within an
event loop, and the result will be returned synchronously.

If the function is called from within an already running event loop, it will
use `asyncio.ensure_future` and `loop.run_until_complete` to execute the coroutine
and wait for the result.

Args:
    func (Callable[P, Awaitable[R]]): An asynchronous function to be wrapped.

Returns:
    Callable[P, R]: A synchronous version of the given function.

Example:
    >>> async def async_function(x: int) -> int:
    ...     await asyncio.sleep(1)
    ...     return x * 2
    ...
    >>> sync_function = _make_sync(async_function)
    >>> result = sync_function(5)
    >>> print(result)
    10

functions.name: funcnodes_core.utils.functions.call_sync
documentation:
Calls a function (synchronous or asynchronous) and returns the result synchronously.

This function calls the provided function (`func`) with the given arguments and keyword arguments.
If the function is asynchronous, it is wrapped in a synchronous function using `_make_sync`.
The result of the function is returned synchronously.

Args:
    func (Callable[P, Union[R, Awaitable[R]]]): The function to be called, which can be synchronous or asynchronous.
    *args: Positional arguments to pass to the function.
    **kwargs: Keyword arguments to pass to the function.

Returns:
    R: The result of the function call.

Example:
    >>> async def async_function(x: int) -> int:
    ...     await asyncio.sleep(1)
    ...     return x * 2
    ...
    >>> result = _call_sync(async_function, 5)
    >>> print(result)
    10

functions.name: funcnodes_core.utils.functions.make_async_if_needed
documentation:
Ensures that a given function is asynchronous.

If the provided function is already asynchronous, it is returned as is.
If the function is synchronous, it is wrapped in an asynchronous function
using `_make_async`.

Args:
    func (Callable[P, Union[R, Awaitable[R]]]): A synchronous or asynchronous function.

Returns:
    Callable[P, Awaitable[R]]: An asynchronous function.

Example:
    >>> def sync_function(x: int) -> int:
    ...     return x * 2
    ...
    >>> async_function = make_async_if_needed(sync_function)
    >>> result = await async_function(5)
    >>> print(result)
    10

    >>> async def already_async_function(x: int) -> int:
    ...     return x * 2
    ...
    >>> same_function = make_async_if_needed(already_async_function)
    >>> result = await same_function(5)
    >>> print(result)
    10

functions.name: funcnodes_core.utils.functions.make_sync_if_needed
documentation:
Ensures that a given function is synchronous.

If the provided function is asynchronous, it is wrapped in a synchronous
function using `_make_sync`. If the function is already synchronous, it
is returned as is.

Args:
    func (Callable[P, Union[R, Awaitable[R]]]): A synchronous or asynchronous function.

Returns:
    Callable[P, R]: A synchronous function.

Example:
    >>> async def async_function(x: int) -> int:
    ...     await asyncio.sleep(1)
    ...     return x * 2
    ...
    >>> sync_function = make_sync_if_needed(async_function)
    >>> result = sync_function(5)
    >>> print(result)
    10

    >>> def already_sync_function(x: int) -> int:
    ...     return x * 2
    ...
    >>> same_function = make_sync_if_needed(already_sync_function)
    >>> result = same_function(5)
    >>> print(result)
    10

functions.name: funcnodes_core.utils.functions.make_run_in_new_thread
documentation:
Wraps a function (synchronous or asynchronous) to run in a new thread with its own ThreadPoolExecutor.

This function returns an `ThreadExecutorWrapper` object that manages the execution of the provided
function in a separate thread. The returned wrapper is callable and returns an awaitable result.

Args:
    func (Callable[P, Union[R, Awaitable[R]]]): The function to be wrapped, which can be synchronous
        or asynchronous.

Returns:
    ThreadExecutorWrapper[P, Awaitable[R]]: An `ThreadExecutorWrapper` object that runs the function
        in a new thread.

Example:
    >>> def blocking_function(x: int) -> int:
    ...     import time
    ...     time.sleep(1)
    ...     return x * 2
    ...
    >>> async_function = make_run_in_new_thread(blocking_function)
    >>> result = await async_function(5)
    >>> print(result)  # Output: 10
    ...
    >>> async_function.shutdown()  # Ensure that the executor is properly shut down

functions.name: funcnodes_core.utils.functions.make_run_in_new_process
documentation:
Wraps a function (synchronous or asynchronous) to run in a new process with its own ProcessPoolExecutor.

This function returns a `ProcessExecutorWrapper` object that manages the execution of the provided
function in a separate process. The returned wrapper is callable and returns an awaitable result.

Args:
    func (Callable[P, Union[R, Awaitable[R]]]): The function to be wrapped, which can be synchronous
        or asynchronous.

Returns:
    ProcessExecutorWrapper[P, Awaitable[R]]: A `ProcessExecutorWrapper` object that runs the function
        in a new process.

Example:
    >>> def cpu_bound_function(x: int) -> int:
    ...     return sum(i * i for i in range(x))
    ...
    >>> async_function = make_run_in_new_process(cpu_bound_function)
    >>> result = await async_function(1000000)
    >>> print(result)
    ...
    >>> async_function.shutdown()  # Ensure that the executor is properly shut down

classes.name: funcnodes_core.utils.functions.ExecutorWrapper

methods.name: funcnodes_core.utils.functions.ExecutorWrapper.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the ExecutorWrapper with the given function.

Args:
    func (Callable[P, R]): The function to be wrapped, which can be synchronous or asynchronous.

The function is converted to a synchronous version if necessary using `make_sync_if_needed`,
and a new executor (ThreadPoolExecutor or ProcessPoolExecutor) is created to manage the execution
of the function in a separate thread or process.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.utils.functions.ExecutorWrapper.__del__
documentation:
Cleans up the ThreadPoolExecutor when the ExecutorWrapper is destroyed.

This method ensures that the executor is properly shut down when the `ExecutorWrapper`
object is garbage collected, preventing resource leaks.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.utils.functions.ExecutorWrapper.shutdown
documentation:
Manually shuts down the ThreadPoolExecutor.

This method allows explicit control over the lifecycle of the executor. It should be called
when the wrapper is no longer needed to ensure that all threads are properly cleaned up.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.utils.functions.ExecutorWrapper.run_in_executor
documentation:
Runs the wrapped function in a new thread or process and returns an awaitable result.

This method submits the wrapped function to the executor and returns a
future that can be awaited. The function is executed in a separate thread or process, allowing
it to run concurrently with other operations.

Args:
    *args: Positional arguments to pass to the wrapped function.
    **kwargs: Keyword arguments to pass to the wrapped function.

Returns:
    Awaitable[R]: An awaitable result of the wrapped function's execution.

Example:
    >>> result = await wrapper.run_in_thread(5)
    >>> print(result)  # Output: 10

methods.name: funcnodes_core.utils.functions.ExecutorWrapper.__call__
documentation:
Allows the wrapper to be called like a function.

This method redirects the call to `run_in_thread`, making the `ExecutorWrapper` object
callable. It simplifies the usage by allowing the wrapper to be used as if it were
the original function, but with the added behavior of running in a new thread or process.

Args:
    *args: Positional arguments to pass to the wrapped function.
    **kwargs: Keyword arguments to pass to the wrapped function.

Returns:
    Awaitable[R]: An awaitable result of the wrapped function's execution.

Example:
    >>> result = await wrapper(5)
    >>> print(result)  # Output: 10

A wrapper that runs a function (synchronous or asynchronous) in a new thread or process
with its own ThreadPoolExecutor or ProcessPoolExecutor.

The `ExecutorWrapper` class wraps a function and ensures that it runs in a new thread
or process managed by a specific executor. The class allows the wrapped function to be called
asynchronously and handles the lifecycle of the thread or process pool.

Attributes:
    syncfunc (Callable[P, R]): The synchronous version of the wrapped function.
    executor (Union[ThreadPoolExecutor, ProcessPoolExecutor]): The executor managing the threads or processes.

Methods:
    __init__(func: Callable[P, R]):
        Initializes the wrapper with the given function and creates the appropriate executor.
    __del__():
        Cleans up the executor when the wrapper is destroyed.
    shutdown():
        Shuts down the executor manually.
    run_in_thread(*args: P.args, **kwargs: P.kwargs) -> Awaitable[R]:
        Runs the wrapped function in a new thread or process and returns an awaitable result.
    __call__(*args: P.args, **kwargs: P.kwargs) -> Awaitable[R]:
        Calls the `run_in_thread` method, allowing the wrapper to be invoked like a function.

classes.name: funcnodes_core.utils.functions.ThreadExecutorWrapper
documentation:
A wrapper that runs a function (synchronous or asynchronous) in a new thread with a ThreadPoolExecutor.

This class is a subclass of `ExecutorWrapper` that specifically uses a `ThreadPoolExecutor`
to run the wrapped function in a separate thread.

to run the wrapped function in a separate process.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.utils.serialization.JSONDecoder.__init__
documentation: Initializes a new JSONDecoder object.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.utils.serialization.JSONDecoder.add_decoder
documentation:
Adds a new decoder to the list of decoders.

Args:
  dec (Callable[[VALID_JSON_TYPE], tuple[Any, bool]]): A function that takes in a valid JSON type and
    returns a tuple containing the decoded object and a boolean indicating whether
    or not the object was decoded.

Examples:
  >>> JSONDecoder.add_decoder(complex_decoder)

Custom JSON decoder that uses a list of decoders to decode JSON objects.

Args:
  object_hook (Callable): A function that will be called with the result of any object literal decoded (a dict).
    The return value of object_hook will be used instead of the dict. This feature can be used to implement
    custom decoders (e.g. JSON-RPC class hinting).

Examples:
  >>> JSONDecoder().decode('{"__complex__": true}', object_hook=complex_decoder)
  (1+2j)

classes.name: funcnodes_core.utils.serialization.JSONEncoder

methods.name: funcnodes_core.utils.serialization.JSONEncoder.add_encoder
documentation:
Adds a new encoder to the list of encoders.

Args:
  enc (encodertyoe): A function that takes in an object and a boolean indicating whether
    or not to use a default preview and returns a tuple containing the encoded object and a
    boolean indicating whether or not the object was encoded.
  enc_cls (Optional[List[type]]): A list of classes that the encoder should be applied to primarily.
Examples:
  >>> def complex_encoder(obj, preview=False):
  ...     if isinstance(obj, complex):
  ...         return {"__complex__": True}, True
  ...     return obj, False
  >>> JSONEncoder.add_encoder(complex_encoder)

methods.name: funcnodes_core.utils.serialization.JSONEncoder.prepend_encoder
documentation:
Adds a new encoder to the list of encoders.

Args:
  enc (encodertyoe): A function that takes in an object and a boolean indicating whether
    or not to use a default preview and returns a tuple containing the encoded object and
    a boolean indicating whether or not the object was encoded.

Examples:
  >>> def complex_encoder(obj, preview=False):
  ...     if isinstance(obj, complex):
  ...         return {"__complex__": True}, True
  ...     return obj, False
  >>> JSONEncoder.add_encoder(complex_encoder)

methods.name: funcnodes_core.utils.serialization.JSONEncoder.apply_custom_encoding

methods.name: funcnodes_core.utils.serialization.JSONEncoder.default

Custom JSON encoder that uses a list of encoders to encode JSON objects.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.node.NodeMeta.__new__
documentation:
Construct and return a new `Node` object. This is the metaclass for all Node types.
This custom implementation of `__new__` is designed to automatically register the new class
in a registry for Node types.

Args:
    mcls: The metaclass instance.
    name (str): The name of the new class.
    bases (Tuple[type, ...]): A tuple of the base classes for the new class.
    namespace (Dict[str, Any]): The namespace containing the definitions for the new class.
    /: Indicates that the preceding arguments are positional-only.
    **kwargs: Extra keyword arguments.

Returns:
    Type[Node]: A new class object of type Node.

Raises:
    TypeError: If the new class is not a subclass of `Node`.
    NameError: If the class is `Node` itself and not an instance of it.

documentation:

Args:
    node_output (NodeOutput): The NodeOutput object to remove.

Returns:
    None

methods.name: funcnodes_core.node.Node.on_nodeio_event
documentation:
Handles events emitted by the node's inputs and outputs.

Args:
    event (str): The event name.
    src (NodeInput | NodeOutput): The source of the event.
    **data: The event data.

Returns:
    None

methods.name: funcnodes_core.node.Node.inputs_ready
documentation: Whether all the node's inputs are ready.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.node.Node.get_input
documentation:
Returns the input with the given uuid.

Args:
    uuid (str): The uuid of the input to return.

Returns:
    NodeInput: The input with the given uuid.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.node.Node.get_output
documentation:
Returns the output with the given uuid.

Args:
    uuid (str): The uuid of the output to return.

Returns:
    NodeOutput: The output with the given uuid.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.node.Node.get_input_or_output
documentation:
Returns the input or output with the given uuid.

Args:
    uuid (str): The uuid of the input or output to return.

Returns:
    NodeInput | NodeOutput: The input or output with the given uuid.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.node.Node.__call__
documentation:
Executes the node's function asynchronously and returns an asyncio.Task object.
This method also handles the triggering of events before and after the function execution.

Returns:
    asyncio.Task: The task object representing the asynchronous operation of the node's function.
endregion input/output methods
region triggering

methods.name: funcnodes_core.node.Node.trigger_if_requested
documentation:
Triggers the node if it is ready to be triggered and a trigger has been requested.

Args:
    triggerstack (Optional[TriggerStack]): The trigger stack to use for the operation, if any.

Returns:
    bool: Whether the node was triggered.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.node.Node.request_trigger
documentation:
Requests the node to be triggered.
If the node is ready to trigger, it triggers it, otherwise it sets the _requests_trigger attribute to True.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.node.Node.await_trigger
documentation:
Asynchronously waits for the node to be ready to trigger and then triggers it
or if node is in trigger waits for the trigger process to finish.

Returns:
    The result of the trigger operation.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.node.Node.await_until_complete
documentation: Asynchronously runs the node until completion.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.node.Node.__await__
documentation:
Allows an instance of Node to be awaited. This will request the node to be triggered and
waits for the trigger process to finish.
Yields:
    The result of awaiting the last task in the stack.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.node.Node.trigger
documentation:
Triggers the node's execution. If the node is already in a trigger state, it raises an InTriggerError.

Args:
    triggerstack (Optional[TriggerStack]): The trigger stack to use for the operation, if any.

Returns:
    TriggerStack: The trigger stack associated with the node's execution.

Raises:
    InTriggerError: If the node is already in a trigger state.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.node.Node.cleanup
documentation: endregion triggering

The base class for all nodes, making use of the custom metaclass `NodeMeta` to handle
automatic registration. It inherits from `EventEmitterMixin` for event handling capabilities
and from `ABC` to allow abstract methods, making it suitable for creating a variety of node types.

classes.name: funcnodes_core.node.NodeReadyState
documentation: A dictionary containing the ready state of a node

classes.name: funcnodes_core.node.NodeStatus
documentation:
A dictionary containing the status of a node.

Attributes:
    ready (bool): Whether the node is ready.
    in_trigger (bool): Whether the node is in trigger.
    requests_trigger (bool): Whether the node requests a trigger.
    inputs (Dict[str, NodeInputStatus]): The status of the node's inputs.
    outputs (Dict[str, NodeOutputStatus]): The status of the node's outputs.

classes.name: funcnodes_core.node.SerializedNodeClass
documentation:
A dictionary containing the serialized data of a node.

Attributes:
    node_id (str): The id of the node.
    node_name (str): The name of the node.
    inputs (Dict[str, NodeInput]): The inputs of the node.
    outputs (Dict[str, NodeOutput]): The outputs of the node.
    description (Optional[str]): The description of the node.
    reset_inputs_on_trigger (bool): Whether to reset the inputs of the node when it is triggered.

classes.name: funcnodes_core.node.NodeKeyError
documentation: Exception raised when a node with a given id is not registered.

classes.name: funcnodes_core.node.PlaceHolderNode
documentation: endregion node registry

classes.name: funcnodes_core.nodespace.NodeException
documentation: Base exception class for node exceptions.

classes.name: funcnodes_core.nodespace.FullNodeSpaceJSON
documentation: FullNodeSpaceJSON for a full serilization including temporary properties

classes.name: funcnodes_core.nodespace.NodeSpaceJSON
documentation: NodeSpaceJSON is the interface for the serialization of a NodeSpace

Handles events emitted by nodes in the NodeSpace.

Removes a node instance from the NodeSpace.

Returns:

Adds a new node instance to the NodeSpace using its ID.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.nodespace.NodeSpace.remove_node_by_id

Removes a node from the nodespace by its id.

Args:

Returns:

Adds a shelf to the nodespace's library.

region lib

methods.name: funcnodes_core.nodespace.NodeSpace.remove_shelf
documentation:
Removes a shelf from the nodespace's library.
This also removes all nodes in the shelf from the nodespace if they are not used in other shelves.

Args:
  shelf (Shelf): The shelf to remove.
    with_nodes (bool): Whether to remove the nodes in the shelf from the nodespace. Defaults to True.

Returns:
  Library: The updated library.

methods.name: funcnodes_core.nodespace.NodeSpace.await_done
documentation: await_done waits until all nodes are done
endregion lib

NodeSpace is a manager and container for nodes and edges between them.
Also it contains a reference to a library of nodes.
